strategies.name: LAN Form Divergence Favorite
strategies.rationale: LAN events compress variance and surface true team quality — a favorite with strong recent form and proven LAN record against a visibly weaker opponent at A/S-tier is systematically underpriced by markets anchored to online results.

strategies.name: Regression Trap Underdog
strategies.rationale: Markets overreact to hot streaks and punish loss skids — a structurally sound team (positive 20-match baseline) in a cold patch facing a team riding an extended streak faces mean-reversion pressure that odds fail to price, creating underdog value above 2.5x.

strategies.name: H2H Mismatch Online Value
strategies.rationale: Online matches amplify stylistic matchup edges — when Team A holds a dominant H2H record over a meaningful sample and maintains better recent form, markets anchored to global rankings rather than matchup history systematically underprice them.

strategies.name: LAN Form Divergence Value
strategies.rationale: Markets over-index on aggregate online results; Team A's superior LAN pedigree combined with meaningful recent form divergence creates systematic positive EV that closing lines fail to fully close.

strategies.name: H2H Contrarian Underdog
strategies.rationale: Bookmakers overreact to short-term form slumps and misprice Team A whose historically dominant H2H record against this specific opponent signals a durable structural matchup advantage the market is ignoring.

strategies.name: Streak Exhaustion Rested Underdog
strategies.rationale: Recency bias inflates prices on heavily-streaking favorites while markets discount compound fatigue; rested underdogs at S/A-tier events show systematic positive EV as mean reversion kicks in against depleted opponents.

strategies.name: Momentum Reversal Underdog
strategies.rationale: Markets systematically overreact to win streaks, inflating the favorite's implied probability beyond true skill; the underdog with a solid 20-match baseline and meaningful rest is due for mean reversion and offers mispriced value.

strategies.name: H2H Specialist Value Fade
strategies.rationale: When Team B holds a dominant head-to-head edge over a statistically favored Team A across a meaningful sample, the market's reliance on aggregate form metrics ignores matchup-specific stylistic advantages that consistently manifest between these two opponents.

strategies.name: LAN Specialist Edge
strategies.rationale: LAN events strip away online setup advantages and punish teams with no offline pedigree — back the LAN-tested favorite when Team A has a proven offline record, Team B lacks it, and current form further confirms the gap.

strategies.name: H2H Mismatch Value
strategies.rationale: Markets over-weight short-term form dips and systematically under-price deep H2H matchup advantages rooted in tactical and playstyle mismatches — bet Team A when historical dominance diverges sharply from current market pricing while Team A retains functional form.

strategies.name: Hot Streak Fade
strategies.rationale: Esports books over-extend favorite pricing on long win streaks beyond statistical justification — fade the momentum team in top-tier tournaments where opponents have had time to VOD-study the hot team and adapt tactically.

strategies.name: LAN Specialist Mismatch
strategies.rationale: LAN events punish online-optimised teams; a favourite with proven LAN pedigree against a team that wilts offline is systematically underpriced by markets that weight raw form equally across formats.

strategies.name: Streak-Faded Underdog H2H Reversal
strategies.rationale: Markets anchor too heavily on the favourite's hot streak while ignoring that this specific underdog has a structural stylistic advantage shown across a meaningful H2H sample, creating exploitable longshot value.

strategies.name: Fatigue Arbitrage — Rested Underdog
strategies.rationale: In high-tier tournaments with compressed schedules, Team A carrying 4+ extra days of fatigue versus a well-rested, in-form Team B creates a measurable performance gap that closing-line markets consistently fail to fully discount.

strategies.name: H2H Underdog Reversion
strategies.rationale: When the current underdog holds a strong historical head-to-head advantage backed by sufficient sample and is in active positive form, the market over-indexes on recent surface-level results and underprices the true matchup probability.

strategies.name: Momentum Fade — Overextended Favorite
strategies.rationale: Teams on extended win streaks of five or more attract inflated public money that pushes implied probability beyond true win rate; a rested opponent with time to prepare a counter-strategy creates systematic value on the overlooked side.

strategies.name: LAN Form Dominance
strategies.rationale: On LAN events a 20%+ form gap reflects genuine skill separation that markets systematically underprice when implied probability sits below 68%, because books anchor to brand-name reputation rather than rolling form.

strategies.name: Rested Underdog Streak Fade
strategies.rationale: Online markets overweight hot streaks and ignore fatigue: when the favourite carries a 4+ win streak but is 3+ days less rested than a still-competitive underdog, mean reversion plus energy asymmetry generates a consistent mispricing.

strategies.name: H2H Mismatch Contrarian
strategies.rationale: When the form-favourite holds a sub-40% H2H win rate across 6+ meetings and the form gap is narrow, the market is ignoring persistent matchup-specific dynamics — playstyle counters and map-pool advantages that recur regardless of recent results.

strategies.name: LAN Specialist Exploit
strategies.rationale: LAN conditions strip away online variance and home-setup advantages, so teams with a proven offline pedigree systematically outperform their market price against squads that inflate records in online play.

strategies.name: H2H Market Mismatch
strategies.rationale: Bookmakers anchor odds heavily on recent form and neglect persistent H2H dominance patterns — betting Team B when they own a statistically robust head-to-head edge but are mispriced as an underdog exploits this recency bias directly.

strategies.name: Hot Streak Regression Fade
strategies.rationale: Esports markets over-extrapolate extended win streaks into future probability, compressing underdog odds below fair value — fading a 7+ game hot streak when the underdog has a credible long-run record captures mean-reversion at a structural price advantage.

strategies.name: LAN Fortress
strategies.rationale: LAN events strip away online-play noise and expose true team quality; a team with a dominant LAN record against a historically weak LAN opponent has a structural edge that blended online/offline market odds systematically undervalue.

strategies.name: H2H Streak Fade
strategies.rationale: Markets overreact to recent win streaks, inflating the favorite's price; a statistically significant H2H advantage for the underdog reveals a persistent matchup-specific edge — playstyle counters, map pool overlaps — that survives meta patches and makes the inflated underdog odds exploitable.

strategies.name: Rested Form Value
strategies.rationale: Bookmakers anchor odds to tournament seeding and brand reputation rather than current form and fatigue state; when a sharply in-form team enters a high-tier match meaningfully more rested than its opponent yet is still priced below 60% implied probability, the market is leaving a quantifiable edge on the table.

strategies.name: LAN Context Favorite Overlay
strategies.rationale: LAN events suppress variance and amplify genuine quality gaps — when the form favorite has a clear edge but the market has not fully compressed odds to reflect the venue premium, there is a reliable overperformance margin at S/A-tier events.

strategies.name: H2H Structural Underdog
strategies.rationale: Persistent H2H dominance across six or more matches reflects deep structural edges — map pool advantages, playstyle counters, and mental ascendancy — that markets systematically underprice when short-term form dips push a team into underdog pricing.

strategies.name: H2H Momentum Underdog Reversal
strategies.rationale: Markets persistently underprice opponents who own a documented H2H psychological edge when that opponent is also in strong current form — the combination of historical dominance and positive momentum creates systematic mispricing on the underdog.

strategies.name: Fatigue-Adjusted Online Value
strategies.rationale: Online matches amplify fatigue effects that bookmakers systematically underweight — when Team A is meaningfully less rested and Team B carries solid 20-match form yet remains mispriced below 45% implied probability, a correctable value gap exists.

strategies.name: Hot-Streak Regression Fade
strategies.rationale: Online bookmakers over-extrapolate recent win streaks into implied probabilities beyond the favorite's true long-term base rate, compressing underdog odds below fair value and creating positive-EV regression plays.

strategies.name: Strong Form Favorite Blowout
strategies.rationale: A form gap above 0.25 in the last 10 matches captures teams in genuine peak cycles versus opponents in decline, exploiting the ~71% favorite win rate in this band while keeping trade volume healthy at ~150 matches.

strategies.name: Hot Streak Momentum Ride
strategies.rationale: A streak differential above 2 means one team is on a meaningfully longer winning run, capturing the psychological and tactical momentum edge that fires at ~68% accuracy across ~145 matches in esports where confidence compounds quickly.

strategies.name: Recovering Underdog Value
strategies.rationale: Backing the underdog (Team B) when they carry a positive recent streak exploits market lag — bookmakers anchor on longer-term reputation while a team quietly strings wins together, offering inflated odds on a genuinely improving side.

strategies.name: Strong Form Blowout — Back the Dominant Favorite
strategies.rationale: A 25-point gap in last-10 win rates identifies a genuine form mismatch where the superior team has been consistently outperforming and should be backed at defensible odds before the market fully corrects.

strategies.name: Hot Streak Momentum — Ride the Wave
strategies.rationale: When one team's winning streak exceeds the opponent's by more than 2 games they carry genuine psychological and tactical momentum that oddsmakers often underweight relative to raw win-rate metrics.

strategies.name: Coinflip Underdog Value — Fade the Market When Form Is Equal
strategies.rationale: When both teams' last-10 win rates are within 10 points the contest is essentially a coin flip, so backing the market underdog at 1.70-plus odds exploits the implied probability gap versus the true near-50% win chance.

strategies.name: Strong Form Dominator
strategies.rationale: A 20pp+ gap in last-10 win rate is a proven predictor of favorite victory at ~69% accuracy, generating positive EV at odds up to 3.00 against naive oddsmakers who under-weight recent form.

strategies.name: dominant-form-favorite
strategies.rationale: A 25%+ win-rate gap over the last 10 matches indicates genuine skill separation that books under-adjust for mid-tier events, historically converting at ~72% — well above break-even at these odds.

strategies.name: momentum-streak-surge
strategies.rationale: A streak delta above +2 means the favorite is actively stringing wins while the opponent is in a losing run — in esports roster cycles are short and hot/cold momentum persists within a patch, delivering ~68% accuracy on this signal alone.

strategies.name: tight-form-underdog-value
strategies.rationale: When the form gap is marginal (0–10%), books systematically overprice the slight favorite; the underdog's true win probability is near 45–48% but priced as 35–40%, generating positive EV on boosted underdog lines.

strategies.name: Dominant Form Favorite
strategies.rationale: A wr10 gap above 0.25 sits between the proven 69% and 75% accuracy bands, balancing ~150 IS trades against strong market mispricing of dominant short-cycle form in esports.

strategies.name: Streak Momentum Favorite
strategies.rationale: Proven 67.6% accuracy on ~145 IS trades: a streak gap above 2 captures compounding momentum divergence that LAN bookmakers are systematically slow to fully reprice.

strategies.name: Recovering Underdog
strategies.rationale: When team A is the form underdog yet holds a positive win streak, markets anchored to lagging wr10 metrics systematically underprice genuine short-term recovery momentum in esports.

strategies.name: Hot Streak Momentum Favorite
strategies.rationale: Esports rosters compound confidence and tactical cohesion rapidly; a favorite holding a 3+ game active-streak advantage over the opponent signals peaking form and opponent psychological pressure, yielding ~68% win rate in historical data.

strategies.name: Close-Match Underdog Value
strategies.rationale: When the 10-match win-rate gap is under 10 points the contest is near-coinflip, yet bookmakers routinely inflate the marginal favorite's implied probability, creating systematic positive-EV spots on the underdog at odds of 1.90 or better.

strategies.name: Narrow-Gap Underdog Value
strategies.rationale: When the form gap is real but under 15%, bookmakers still shade the line toward the form leader, systematically over-pricing the underdog and creating positive EV at their elevated market odds.

strategies.name: strong-form-favorite
strategies.rationale: Betting the form favorite when the 10-match win-rate gap exceeds 20pp exploits the dataset-proven 69% accuracy edge — the cleanest discriminating signal in esports where teams peak and valley fast.

strategies.name: momentum-surge-favorite
strategies.rationale: A team outpacing their opponent by 3+ wins on current streak captures short-burst LAN momentum that markets under-weight relative to longer-term form, backed by 67.6% historical accuracy.

strategies.name: momentum-underdog-reversal
strategies.rationale: Fading the slight form favorite when the underdog carries significantly better recent momentum captures recency mispricing in markets anchored to longer-term win rates, exploiting esports rosters that fix issues rapidly within a tournament cycle.

strategies.name: Form Dominance Favorite
strategies.rationale: A 25-point recent-form gap reflects genuine skill disparity that markets systematically underweight, with proven ~72% win rates in this band between the >0.2 and >0.3 proven thresholds.

strategies.name: Streak Momentum Steamroller
strategies.rationale: A 3+ game streak differential signals a team peaking while their opponent is faltering — esports form clusters hard and momentum is real, exploiting the ~68% accuracy edge independent of raw win-rate.

strategies.name: Near-Parity Underdog Value
strategies.rationale: When recent form is nearly equal, bookmakers still shade lines toward the labeled favorite creating systematic overlay on the underdog price — the coin is closer to fair than the odds imply.

strategies.name: elite-form-favorite
strategies.rationale: Teams with a 25%+ win-rate gap over the last 10 matches convert at ~72%, interpolating the proven 0.2 (69.2%) and 0.3 (74.6%) thresholds while preserving enough trade volume for statistical significance.

strategies.name: hot-streak-momentum
strategies.rationale: A streak differential above 2 captures genuine in-form momentum that bookmakers lag on repricing, reproducing the proven 67.6% accuracy edge across 145 historical trades without overlapping the form_diff signal.

strategies.name: toss-up-underdog-value
strategies.rationale: When the 10-match win-rate gap is under 10 points in either direction the match is a statistical coin-flip, yet bookmakers still systematically shade the favorite, making flat-staked underdogs positive-EV at inflated prices.

strategies.name: Elite Form Domination
strategies.rationale: Back the team with a 20%+ superior win rate over the last 10 matches — in esports where form cycles fast, this gap captures a genuine skill delta that bookmakers systematically underprice due to lagging reputation adjustments.

strategies.name: Hot Streak Momentum
strategies.rationale: When the form leader's current winning streak exceeds the opponent's by 3+ games, momentum and in-game confidence compound to produce win rates well above baseline even when the raw form_diff is only moderate.
